prompt: I want to measure whether my new arc-debugging skill actually changes agent behavior versus no skill. How should I design an eval scenario to test this?
targets: Preflight + ceiling check + answer-leakage awareness + discriminative trap design + grader-type matching

assertions.text: Response recommends paired runs (with-skill vs without-skill) at k≥5
assertions.note: Both likely cover this — standard A/B hygiene

assertions.text: Response mentions preflight OR baseline-ceiling check as a scenario-validity step
Response mentions answer leakage / prompt leaking the skill's keywords ('hypothesis', 'reproduce', 'root cause')
Response names 'discriminative trap' or 'bait' as scenario design element (not just 'failure mode')
Response prescribes grader-type matching to assertion nature (code for deterministic, model for judgment)
Response starts with scope alignment (skill vs agent vs workflow eval)
assertions.note: Treatment should, baseline typically skips directly to mechanics

assertions.text: Response recommends one-behavior-per-scenario (prefer 3-5 narrow scenarios over one overloaded)
